reasoning: The market price on October 30, 2025, was 14%, which serves as a strong base rate for a strike in the final two months of the year. Recent simulated news from November 2025 indicates that de-escalation is occurring on multiple fronts: Israel is withdrawing forces from Gaza under a ceasefire deal (with a hostage exchange on Nov 2) and US Special Envoy Thomas Barrack is mediating negotiations in Lebanon. Given that the regional conflict is cooling down and massive strikes on Iran already took place earlier in the year (June 2025), the likelihood of a fresh Israeli strike on Iran before December 31, 2025, has likely decreased from the late-October estimate. I am explicitly updating down from the 14% anchor to 11% to reflect the ongoing ceasefires.
